methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Persona AI is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Nic Radford and Jerry Pratt that has raised $25M to date. The robotics and manufacturing sector comprises 9 peer companies—1 public and 8 private—including comparable-stage entities like Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

Given the early stage and limited availability of seed-stage comparables within the robotics subsector, peer-median valuation proved inapplicable; the narrow peer set yielded fewer than three datapoints meeting stage and tier criteria necessary for reliable triangulation. Consequently, stage-progression methodology was applied as the primary valuation anchor. Using the $25M total raised as a value proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $62M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $53M (low) to $72M (high), centered on $62M. This range reflects a ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression result, acknowledging execution risk inherent to seed-stage companies and the absence of corroborating peer data. Key uncertainties include near-term fundraising dynamics, the strength of the underlying technology relative to the competitive set, and macro conditions affecting venture capital availability in robotics. Additional traction data or a broader peer comparable set would materially improve conviction in this estimate.
